Propose how you would convert an enantiomer of 2-phenylcyclopropanecarboxylic acid (which is made from trans-cinnamic acid) to tranylcypromine. (Hint: If someone is asking you to rearrange your atoms, it is best to be courteous.). What reaction would you use to effect the first transformation written below? Trans-cinnamic acid is O=C(O)/C=C/c1ccccc1, 2-phenylcyclopropanecarboxylic acid is [H][C@]1(C(=O)O)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2, tranylcypromine is [H][C@]1(N)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2.
